headline_verdict.safe_claim: No continuous hidden anti-fit parameter was found. H_ext(alpha,n) is DERIVED/SCOPED within the adopted stellar-photometric and centered lens-normalization extensions on the Paper 10 legacy H0=67.57585653582628 baseline.
headline_verdict.main_caveat: Paper 34 v1.0 should make the extension premises and legacy-baseline branch explicit under Conventions v2.0 before any future v1.1.

candidate_fields.replacement_family: h in H_ext = h * f_Gamma^(1-alpha) * x^((n/2)K_gauge)
candidate_fields.rigidity_result: Not fit to the six H0 methods; inherited from the Paper 10 legacy projected observer baseline. Later branch audits distinguish this from the Schur 68.91 branch.
candidate_fields.visibility_issue: Future edits should state the legacy baseline branch explicitly.

candidate_fields.rigidity_result: Standalone value forced by Q=1+gamma^2 and f_Gamma=Q^(-1). Its use as J_alpha ladder is conditional on projector placement.
candidate_fields.classification: DERIVED as constant; DERIVED/CONDITIONAL in H_ext projector placement

candidate_fields.rigidity_result: Within the stellar-photometric extension, the centered scalar boundary/KMS action on log-frequency is generated by K_gauge, yielding J_L=x^(-K_gauge).
The sign and 1-alpha exponent follow from inverse-estimator algebra once J_alpha=f_Gamma^(alpha-1) is admitted.
The 1/2 follows from flux-distance inversion F=L/(4piD^2); H inferred scales as J_L^(-1/2).
n is count of uncancelled stellar-photometric calibration legs after shared-source cancellation and primitive-geometry trivialization.
Planck H0 is treated as no-single-alpha composite CMB extraction tracking the projected baseline. Encoding as alpha=1,n=0 is shorthand only.
GW sirens are direct amplitude-distance measurements with no stellar photometric legs and no Fermat lens normalization.
Inside the stellar-photometric extension, direct TRGB is one threshold standard-candle luminosity functional plus one uncancelled photometric leg.
candidate_fields.classification: DERIVED/SCOPED within stellar-photometric extension; otherwise RECONSTRUCTION

candidate_fields.rigidity_result: Inside the stellar-photometric extension, Cepheid+SN is a nested two-candle photometric ladder with a bilocal ladder projector.
candidate_fields.classification: DERIVED/SCOPED within stellar-photometric extension; otherwise RECONSTRUCTION
candidate_fields.anti_fit_risk: highest, because assigned grid point is also the best numerical grid point

candidate_fields.rigidity_result: TRGB+SN follows the SN-ladder architecture rather than direct TRGB, so it inherits the two-leg nested photometric structure.
candidate_fields.classification: DERIVED/SCOPED within stellar-photometric extension; otherwise RECONSTRUCTION

candidate_fields.rigidity_result: Inside centered lens-normalization extension, Weyl/Fermat gives alpha=2, projective centering gives lambda_MST=1, SO(3)-centering gives beta_ani=0, and no stellar-photometric chain gives n=0.
candidate_fields.classification: DERIVED/SCOPED within centered lens-normalization extension; otherwise RECONSTRUCTION

candidate_fields.rigidity_result: Product structure follows from independent inverse-estimator and photometric factors plus no-leakage theorems within the combined extension package.

anti_fit_backstop.reason: Planck is a baseline/no-single-alpha composite CMB extraction, not selected by alpha fitting.
The structurally assigned direct-geometry row is not the best numerical grid point.
Assignment is structurally threshold-candle plus one photometric leg inside extension; numerical best match alone is not used as derivation.
Assignment follows Weyl/Fermat class plus centered lens-normalization selectors.
Assignment rests on Cepheid+SN two-leg ladder derivation inside extension; because it is also the best numerical grid point, the derivation path must remain public and explicit.
TRGB+SN is classified by the SN-ladder architecture, not by direct TRGB alone.

premises: A1 scalar radiative bridge carrier h_phot^br admitted
A2 Cepheid and TRGB source maps live on calibrator-specific descendants
A3 primitive standard-candle readout is band-limited luminosity functional and D_L geometry is projector-trivial
A4 scalar centered boundary/KMS action on log-frequency line is generated by K_gauge
B2 non-geometric TDCOSMO class is alpha=2 Weyl/Fermat
B3 boundary state remains centered and SO(3)-equivariant on auxiliary normalization families
B4 selector acts before per-lens or hyperpopulation marginalization

claim_boundaries: Do not describe H_ext as an unconditional universal all-method H0 theorem.
Do not hide that H0=67.57585653582628 is the Paper 10 legacy projected baseline branch.
Do not say the old stack alone derives the stellar-photometric bridge.
Do not say the old stack alone derives centered lens-normalization selectors.
It is safe to say no continuously fitted alpha/n/kappa parameter was found.
It is safe to say the scorecard is zero-continuous-fit within the stated extension package.
